It modifies the stored data for the object, including fields and metadata such as owner and timestamps. Ensure the object ID and record ID are correct to avoid updating the wrong entry.

e9dadfb2cd1ad128.summary: Retrieve a list of all departments in the HRIS system
e9dadfb2cd1ad128.description: This operation fetches a complete list of departments within the HRIS system. Use this endpoint when you need to view or process all department records rather than a specific one. It reads department data without modifying any records. There are no special constraints beyond standard access permissions.

e0314d824fecd6b2.summary: Copy a folder to a specified location with an optional new name
e0314d824fecd6b2.description: This operation copies an existing folder to a new location within the file storage system, optionally assigning it a new name. Use this when you need to duplicate folder contents without affecting the original. It creates a separate copy under the specified parent folder, preserving the original folder and its contents. Ensure the target location allows folder creation and that the new name does not conflict with existing folders.

c96c826fdf4017ca.summary: Retrieve details of a specific ecommerce store
c96c826fdf4017ca.description: This operation fetches detailed information about a single ecommerce store. Use it when you need to view or verify store data, such as name, address, or settings. It reads store data without modifying it and does not have side effects. Ensure the store identifier is correctly specified to retrieve the intended store.

e35f37eb2b21b5a5.summary: Upload a file part to an existing upload session
e35f37eb2b21b5a5.description: This operation uploads a segment of a file to a specified upload session, with each part limited to a maximum size of 100MB. Use this endpoint when you need to upload large files in parts, ensuring each part (except the last) matches the expected part size obtained from the upload session details. It writes the file data to the session and requires the base URL upload.apideck.com. Ensure the Content-Length header matches the part size for all parts except the final one to avoid errors.

9d36da82e5a20c65.summary: Retrieve a list of all customers
9d36da82e5a20c65.description: This operation fetches a complete list of customers in the ecommerce system. Use this endpoint when you need to view or process all customer records rather than individual customer details. It reads customer data but does not modify any records. There are no special constraints beyond standard access permissions.

8bc566bcfdd5678e.summary: Retrieve a list of all DriveGroups
8bc566bcfdd5678e.description: This operation fetches all DriveGroups available in the file storage system. Use this endpoint when you need to view or manage the collection of DriveGroups rather than individual drives or files. It reads the current state of DriveGroups without modifying any data. There are no special constraints beyond standard access permissions.

8c06238c9597c8b3.summary: Retrieve a custom object schema by its ID
8c06238c9597c8b3.description: This operation fetches the details of a specific custom object schema using its unique identifier. Use this endpoint when you need to view the structure and metadata of a particular custom object schema in your CRM. It reads the schema data without modifying it. Ensure the provided ID corresponds to an existing custom object schema to avoid errors.

6eb1b6ef7bd9f270.summary: Update a shared link's settings and access controls
6eb1b6ef7bd9f270.description: This operation modifies the properties of an existing shared link, such as its URL, access scope, password protection, and expiration. Use this endpoint when you need to change how a shared link is configured without creating a new one. It updates the link's metadata and access restrictions, affecting who can view or download the linked content. Ensure that any password or expiration changes comply with your organization's sharing policies.

2df42bbebf6c21db.summary: Upload a file up to 100MB to file storage
2df42bbebf6c21db.description: This operation uploads a single file with a maximum size of 100MB to the file storage system. Use this endpoint for smaller files; for files larger than 100MB, use the Upload Sessions API instead. The upload writes the file data to storage and makes it available for subsequent access. Files exceeding the size limit will be rejected, so ensure your file meets the size constraint before uploading.

baabc89d62a1bf37.summary: Retrieve a folder by its ID
baabc89d62a1bf37.description: This operation fetches the details of a specific folder using its unique identifier. Use this endpoint when you need to access metadata or information about a single folder within the file storage system. It reads folder data without modifying any content. Ensure the folder ID exists to avoid errors.

f79e224e8569706a.summary: Retrieve a DriveGroup by its ID
f79e224e8569706a.description: This operation fetches the details of a specific DriveGroup using its unique identifier. Use this endpoint when you need to view or verify the properties of an existing DriveGroup. It reads and returns the current state of the DriveGroup without modifying any data. Ensure the provided ID corresponds to an existing DriveGroup to avoid errors.

4e16395cfcb05a67.summary: Retrieve a list of all companies in the HRIS system
4e16395cfcb05a67.description: Fetches a complete list of companies managed within the HRIS platform. Use this operation when you need to view or process all company records rather than individual entries. It reads company data without modifying any records. This operation does not support filtering or pagination, so it returns all companies available at the time of the request.

9050bc2c5d5d5fe7.summary: Create a shared link for a file or folder
9050bc2c5d5d5fe7.description: This operation creates a shared link that provides access to a specified file or folder within the file storage system. Use this endpoint when you want to generate a public or company-restricted link to share content securely, optionally protecting it with a password or setting an expiration date. The operation writes the shared link details, including access scope and security settings, to the system. Note that the link can target a specific item by ID or detailed target information, and password protection requires providing the password in the request.

609063f6bd6889d4.summary: Complete an upload session after all file parts are uploaded
609063f6bd6889d4.description: This operation finalizes an upload session once all parts of a file have been successfully uploaded using the corresponding upload parts endpoint. Use this endpoint to signal that the file upload is complete and ready for processing or storage. It writes the final state of the upload session and consolidates the uploaded parts into a single file. Note that this endpoint uses a different base URL (upload.apideck.com) than other API calls and should only be called after all parts are uploaded.

3936d774930de9a0.summary: Retrieve a list of all products
3936d774930de9a0.description: This operation fetches a complete list of products available in the ecommerce system. Use this endpoint when you need to display or process all products rather than a specific subset. It reads product data without modifying any records. There are no special constraints beyond standard access permissions.

535522964911926e.summary: Retrieve a list of all time off requests
535522964911926e.description: Fetches all time off requests recorded in the system. Use this operation to view or manage employee leave data comprehensively. It reads existing time off request records without modifying any data. This endpoint is ideal when you need a full overview rather than details of a single request.

eebbaeeee1db8282.summary: Export a file by its ID
eebbaeeee1db8282.description: This operation exports the contents of a specified file from the file storage system. Use this endpoint when you need to retrieve a file's data for download or transfer. It reads the file data associated with the given ID and returns it in the response. Ensure the file ID exists and you have appropriate access rights to avoid errors.

118d998c66c61a5e.summary: Delete a folder from file storage
118d998c66c61a5e.description: This operation deletes a specified folder from the file storage system. Use this when you need to permanently remove a folder and its contents. Deleting a folder may affect any files or subfolders contained within it, so ensure no important data is lost. This action is irreversible and does not return a response body.

4cbb6ea73c98c996.summary: Retrieve detailed information about a specific company
4cbb6ea73c98c996.description: This operation fetches detailed information about a single company identified by its ID. Use this endpoint when you need to view or verify company details within the HRIS system. It reads company data without modifying it and returns the current state of the company's information. Ensure the company ID exists to avoid errors.

670a3f69103ae6e0.summary: Update properties of a file storage drive by its identifier
670a3f69103ae6e0.description: This operation modifies the attributes of an existing file storage drive, such as its name, description, or custom mappings. Use this endpoint when you need to change drive metadata or configuration details without creating a new drive. It updates the stored data for the specified drive and may affect how the drive is accessed or displayed. Ensure that the drive identifier is correct and that you provide only the fields you intend to update, as unspecified fields may remain unchanged.

d322fb5d16e4353e.summary: Retrieve details of a specific upload session
d322fb5d16e4353e.description: This operation fetches the details of an existing upload session, including the recommended part size for splitting files. Use this endpoint to obtain session information before uploading file parts via the upload part endpoint. It is intended for managing multipart file uploads and helps coordinate the upload process. Note that the upload service uses a different base URL than other API endpoints.

89a50888455d7e8a.summary: Retrieve a list of all payroll records
89a50888455d7e8a.description: Fetches all payroll records available in the system. Use this operation to obtain a comprehensive overview of payroll data rather than individual payroll details or updates. It reads payroll information without modifying any data. This operation does not support filtering or pagination, so it returns the full set of payroll records.

d576db31d372f7e3.summary: Delete a file by its ID
d576db31d372f7e3.description: This operation deletes a file identified by its ID from the file storage system. Use this when you need to permanently remove a file and free up storage space. It does not return any content upon success. Ensure the file ID exists, as deleting a non-existent file may result in an error.

bd73133c47a5cb9b.summary: Create a new file storage drive with specified properties
bd73133c47a5cb9b.description: This operation creates a new drive in the file storage system using the provided details such as name, description, and custom mappings. Use this endpoint when you need to add a new storage drive rather than modifying existing ones or retrieving drive information. It writes new drive data to the system and may include metadata like creation and update timestamps and user information. Ensure that the drive ID and other identifiers are unique to avoid conflicts.

ebec07666c76c662.summary: Delete a shared link by its ID
ebec07666c76c662.description: This operation removes a shared link from the file storage system. Use this when you want to revoke access previously granted via a shared link. Deleting the link immediately invalidates it, preventing further access to the associated file or resource. This action is irreversible and does not delete the underlying file itself.

45fa9f19e4cef417.summary: Delete a DriveGroup by its ID
45fa9f19e4cef417.description: Deletes the specified DriveGroup from the file storage system. Use this operation when you need to permanently remove a DriveGroup and its associations. This action cannot be undone and will remove all references to the DriveGroup. Ensure that the DriveGroup is no longer needed before deletion to avoid data loss.

a3d5c7ccfba19cd7.summary: Retrieve a list of all issue tracking collections
a3d5c7ccfba19cd7.description: Fetches all existing issue tracking collections available in the system. Use this operation when you need an overview of all collections rather than details of a specific one. It reads the current state of collections without modifying any data. There are no special constraints beyond standard access permissions.

deff49d9a2eef7ec.summary: Abort an active file upload session
deff49d9a2eef7ec.description: This operation aborts an ongoing file upload session, terminating the upload process immediately. Use this when you need to cancel an upload in progress, such as when the upload is no longer needed or an error occurs. It stops any further data transfer and releases associated resources. Note that this endpoint uses a different base URL (upload.apideck.com) than other API calls.

33fe52435bda2eb2.summary: Retrieve a list of all SMS messages
33fe52435bda2eb2.description: Fetches a complete list of SMS messages available in the system. Use this operation when you need to view or process all messages rather than a specific message or subset. It reads stored message data without modifying any records. This operation may return large datasets depending on message volume, so consider filtering or pagination if supported elsewhere.

1c8bb0b60fc48f59.summary: Retrieve all payroll records for a specific employee
1c8bb0b60fc48f59.description: Fetches the complete list of payroll entries associated with a given employee. Use this operation when you need to review or analyze all payroll data for an individual employee. It reads payroll information without modifying any data. Ensure the employee identifier is valid to receive accurate results.

0dc155be2a9fcefc.summary: Update an existing employee's details in the HRIS system
0dc155be2a9fcefc.description: This operation modifies the information of a specific employee in the HRIS database. Use this endpoint when you need to change employee attributes such as name, title, or marital status, rather than creating a new employee record. It updates the stored data for the employee identified by the given ID and may affect downstream systems if integrated. Ensure that the employee ID exists, as this operation only updates existing records and does not create new ones.

046265ebf3b3602d.summary: Create a new DriveGroup with specified attributes
046265ebf3b3602d.description: This operation creates a new DriveGroup resource with the provided details such as name, display name, description, and custom mappings. Use this endpoint when you need to add a new DriveGroup to organize or manage file storage drives. It writes the DriveGroup data to the system and may include metadata about creation and updates. Ensure that the provided identifiers and mappings conform to expected formats to avoid errors.

11af22677e84b287.summary: Add a new employee record to the HR system
11af22677e84b287.description: This operation creates a new employee record with the provided personal and employment details. Use this endpoint when you need to register a new employee in the HR system rather than updating existing records. It writes employee data such as names, titles, and identifiers to the database. Ensure all required fields are included, as missing data may prevent successful creation.

65c801ead4a27983.summary: Delete a company by its ID
65c801ead4a27983.description: This operation deletes a company record from the system using its unique identifier. Use this endpoint when you need to permanently remove a company and its associated data. Deleting a company is irreversible and may affect related records or integrations that depend on this company. Ensure that the company ID is valid and that you have appropriate permissions before performing this action.

19529689f92eebce.summary: Retrieve a shared link by its ID
19529689f92eebce.description: This operation fetches the details of a specific shared link using its unique identifier. Use this endpoint when you need to access or verify the properties of an existing shared link rather than creating or modifying one. It reads the shared link data without altering it. Ensure the provided ID corresponds to an existing shared link to avoid errors.

2a44883ea0e7af74.summary: Delete a drive by its identifier
2a44883ea0e7af74.description: This operation deletes a specified drive from the file storage system. Use this when you need to permanently remove a drive and its associated data. Deleting a drive is irreversible and will remove all stored files within that drive. Ensure the drive is no longer needed before performing this action to avoid data loss.

a2ea626eda72154f.summary: Update a comment on a ticket within a collection
a2ea626eda72154f.description: This operation updates the content or metadata of an existing comment on a specific ticket in an issue tracking collection. Use this endpoint when you need to modify comment details such as the body text or custom mappings, rather than creating a new comment or deleting one. It reads and writes comment data including timestamps and author information, ensuring the comment remains associated with the correct ticket and collection. Note that partial updates are supported, and the comment ID must correspond to an existing comment within the specified ticket and collection.

862972d5a18e4b5e.summary: Update properties of an existing DriveGroup
862972d5a18e4b5e.description: Modify the attributes of a specified DriveGroup, such as its name, display name, description, and custom mappings. Use this operation when you need to change metadata or configuration details of a DriveGroup without creating a new one. It updates the stored data for the DriveGroup identified by the given ID and may affect access or organization of associated drives. Ensure that the provided data respects any constraints on field formats and that the DriveGroup ID exists before attempting the update.

26f713c0dda989d7.summary: Retrieve a specific SMS message by its ID
26f713c0dda989d7.description: This operation fetches the details of a single SMS message using its unique identifier. Use this endpoint when you need to view the content or status of a particular message rather than a list of messages. It reads message data without modifying it and requires a valid message ID. This call does not support filtering or pagination since it targets one message only.

4133dc7a5197f4ad.description: This operation modifies the attributes of an existing department within the HRIS system. Use this endpoint when you need to change department information such as its name, code, description, or hierarchical parent. It updates the stored data for the specified department and may affect organizational structure or reporting. Ensure that the department ID is valid and that changes comply with any organizational constraints on department relationships.

e6e8737f416e6a2f.summary: Create a new company record in the HRIS system
e6e8737f416e6a2f.description: This operation creates a new company entry within the HRIS platform. Use this endpoint when you need to add a company with details such as legal name, display name, status, and contact information. It writes the provided company data to the system, establishing a new company profile. Ensure that required fields like company ID and currency are correctly specified to avoid creation errors.

aef7d2a8ebf4d6b2.summary: Create a new department in the HRIS system
aef7d2a8ebf4d6b2.description: This operation creates a new department within the HRIS system, allowing you to define its name, code, description, and hierarchical parent if applicable. Use this endpoint when adding organizational units to reflect company structure. It writes department data including custom mappings and audit fields such as created_by and updated_by. Ensure that unique identifiers and required fields are correctly provided to avoid conflicts or incomplete records.

609f733096b59be5.summary: Retrieve all work schedules for a specific employee
609f733096b59be5.description: This operation fetches all defined work schedules associated with a given employee, representing their planned work patterns rather than actual hours worked. Use this endpoint when you need to view or manage an employee's scheduled work patterns, distinct from time tracking or attendance records. It reads the employee's schedule data and does not modify any records. Ensure the employee identifier is valid to receive accurate schedule information.

b3767c9afde52919.summary: Update a ticket's details within a collection
b3767c9afde52919.description: Modify the attributes of an existing ticket in a specified collection, such as its subject, description, status, priority, and assignees. Use this operation when you need to change ticket information without creating a new ticket or deleting the existing one. It updates the stored ticket data and may affect workflow or notification systems depending on the changes. Ensure the ticket and collection IDs are valid, and note that partial updates are supported via this PATCH method.

43973b33d9d2e723.summary: Delete an employee by their ID
43973b33d9d2e723.description: This operation removes an employee record from the system permanently. Use this endpoint when you need to completely erase an employee's data rather than just deactivate or archive it. Deleting an employee will remove all associated information and cannot be undone, so ensure this action is intentional. This operation does not return a response body.

a3ac3f7bf5c71d6a.summary: Retrieve detailed information about a specific drive
a3ac3f7bf5c71d6a.description: This operation fetches detailed information about a single drive identified by its ID. Use this endpoint when you need to access metadata or status of a particular drive rather than listing multiple drives. It reads the current state of the drive without modifying any data. Ensure the drive ID provided is valid to avoid errors.

b661fc6c55518fb2.description: Modify the information of an existing company in the system using this operation. Use this endpoint when you need to change company attributes such as name, status, contact details, or currency. It updates the stored company record with the provided data, affecting how the company is represented in the HRIS. Ensure the company ID is correct, as this operation targets a specific company and partial updates are supported.

284cb563bbc7f18f.summary: Retrieve details of a specific API by its ID
284cb563bbc7f18f.description: This operation fetches detailed information about a single API identified by its ID. Use this endpoint when you need to view or verify the configuration or metadata of a particular API within the connector system. It reads and returns the stored API data without modifying it. Ensure the specified ID corresponds to an existing API to avoid errors.

9634cef7fe3714c4.summary: Update an existing employee time off request
9634cef7fe3714c4.description: Modify details of a specific time off request for an employee, such as dates, status, or type. Use this operation when you need to change an existing request rather than creating a new one or retrieving information. It updates the stored data for the request, affecting how the time off is tracked and approved. Ensure the request ID and employee ID are correct to avoid unintended changes.

96f14a534b3ae1d9.summary: Retrieve a list of all connectors
96f14a534b3ae1d9.description: Fetches a complete list of connectors available in the system. Use this operation when you need to view or manage all connectors rather than a specific one. It reads connector metadata without modifying any data. This operation does not require any input parameters and returns all connectors currently registered.

9773a435832992c9.summary: Retrieve detailed information about a specific payroll
9773a435832992c9.description: This operation fetches detailed information for a single payroll identified by its ID. Use this endpoint when you need to view or verify payroll details for a particular payroll instance. It reads payroll data without modifying it and is useful for auditing or reporting purposes. Ensure the payroll ID provided exists to avoid errors.

4c3d34c7b095dcb9.summary: Delete a department by its ID
4c3d34c7b095dcb9.description: This operation removes a department from the HRIS system using its unique identifier. Use this endpoint when you need to permanently delete a department record, unlike updating or retrieving department details. Deleting a department may affect related employee or organizational data, so ensure dependencies are handled beforehand. The operation does not return a response body upon success.

d34c74b20c00aa75.summary: Delete an SMS message by its ID
d34c74b20c00aa75.description: This operation deletes a specific SMS message identified by its ID. Use this to remove messages that are no longer needed or to manage message storage. Deleting a message permanently removes it and cannot be undone. This operation does not affect other messages or related data.

0c701f6522e5ce40.summary: Retrieve a specific payroll record for an employee
0c701f6522e5ce40.description: This operation fetches the payroll details for a given employee identified by their employee ID and payroll ID. Use this endpoint when you need to view or verify a particular payroll entry rather than a list of payrolls. It reads payroll data associated with the specified employee and payroll identifiers. Ensure the IDs are valid to avoid errors, as the operation requires both employee and payroll IDs to locate the record.

fee4937b628c88ce.summary: Retrieve detailed information about a specific employee
fee4937b628c88ce.description: This operation fetches comprehensive data for a single employee identified by their unique ID. Use this endpoint when you need to view or verify individual employee details rather than bulk employee data. It reads employee records from the HRIS database without modifying any data. Ensure the employee ID provided exists to avoid errors.

08dd044b7363b06c.summary: Create a new consumer in the vault
08dd044b7363b06c.description: This operation creates a new consumer entity within the vault system. Use this endpoint when you need to register a consumer with specific metadata for identification or access purposes. It writes the consumer data to the vault, making it available for subsequent operations involving that consumer. Ensure the consumer ID is unique to avoid conflicts.

ed8aeff1031379c9.summary: Retrieve all comments for a specific ticket in a collection
ed8aeff1031379c9.description: Fetches the complete list of comments associated with a given ticket within a specified collection. Use this operation when you need to view the discussion or notes related to a particular ticket. It reads all existing comments but does not modify any data. Ensure the collection and ticket identifiers are valid to successfully retrieve the comments.

5c1954bbeafeb7bf.summary: Create a new time off request for an employee
5c1954bbeafeb7bf.description: This operation creates a time off request record for an specified employee under a given policy. Use this endpoint when you need to submit a new request for vacation, sick leave, personal time, or other types of absence. It writes the request details including dates, type, status, and amount of time off. Ensure the request data complies with your organization's policies and that status values reflect the current state of the request.

0ade98b57f1f0cff.summary: Proxy a downstream PUT request with injected credentials for secure forwarding
0ade98b57f1f0cff.description: This operation forwards a PUT request to a downstream service while injecting necessary credentials stored in Vault. Use this when you need an additional security layer and logging without relying on Unify for request normalization. It securely proxies all data to the downstream URL and includes the original method and verb in the headers. Note that the request data is passed through Vault without modification, ensuring credential injection and auditability.

048fde587db249b9.summary: Create a new ticket in a specified issue tracking collection
048fde587db249b9.description: This operation creates a new ticket within a given issue tracking collection. Use this endpoint when you need to add a ticket to organize and track issues related to a specific collection. It writes ticket details such as subject, description, status, priority, and assignees to the collection. Ensure the collection ID is valid, as the ticket will be associated with that collection and cannot exist independently.

063e6e35a3391270.summary: Send a new SMS or MMS message
063e6e35a3391270.description: This operation sends a new SMS or MMS message by creating it with specified details such as sender, recipient, content, and type. Use this endpoint when you need to initiate outbound messages programmatically, unlike endpoints that retrieve or manage existing messages. It records message metadata including status, direction, scheduling, and timestamps, affecting message delivery and tracking. Ensure message type and content comply with allowed formats and that scheduling times are valid if used.

6d7bc7ad8c04066c.summary: Delete a specific time off request for an employee
6d7bc7ad8c04066c.description: This operation deletes a particular time off request associated with an employee. Use this endpoint when you need to remove an existing time off request from the system. Deleting the request permanently removes its record and cannot be undone. Ensure the request ID corresponds to the correct employee to avoid unintended data loss.

964515789aabeb18.summary: Retrieve a specific department by its ID
964515789aabeb18.description: This operation fetches detailed information about a single department using its unique identifier. Use this endpoint when you need to view or verify data for one department rather than a list of departments. It reads department data without modifying it and requires a valid department ID. Ensure the ID corresponds to an existing department to avoid errors.

eb213e18913d0c0a.summary: Retrieve the content of a specific connector document
eb213e18913d0c0a.description: This operation fetches the content of a specified document associated with a connector. Use this when you need to access detailed information or documentation for a particular connector. It reads the document data without modifying any connector settings. Ensure the connector and document IDs are valid to successfully retrieve the content.

d00550054a813471.summary: Retrieve a specific comment from a ticket in an issue tracking collection
d00550054a813471.description: This operation fetches the details of a single comment identified by its ID within a specified ticket and collection. Use this when you need to view or process the content of an individual comment rather than a list of comments. It reads the comment data without modifying it and requires valid collection, ticket, and comment identifiers. Ensure the specified comment exists within the given ticket and collection to avoid errors.

5b85861265579594.summary: Exchange stored credentials for an access token and save it in Vault
5b85861265579594.description: This operation exchanges persisted connection credentials for an access token and stores it securely in Vault. Use this when you need to authorize a connection that uses the client_credentials or password OAuth grant type without sending credentials in the request. It does not return the access token but confirms success with a 200 response, indicating the token is stored for future API requests. This operation does not persist any changes beyond storing the access token and should not include credentials in the request body.

05aff6d1faa2fd52.description: This operation fetches all configured integration connections available in the vault. Use this endpoint to obtain the complete list of integrations for building an integrations page where users can install them. OAuth2-supported integrations include authorize and revoke links to manage authentication flows. This endpoint reads the current integration configurations but does not modify any data.

67746ed0291a07c5.summary: Retrieve a specific issue tracking collection by its ID
67746ed0291a07c5.description: This operation fetches the details of a single issue tracking collection identified by its unique ID. Use this endpoint when you need to view or manage the contents of a particular collection rather than listing all collections. It reads the collection data without modifying it. Ensure the collection ID provided is valid to avoid errors.

191d7ef86f86e36d.summary: Retrieve a specific user from an issue tracking collection
191d7ef86f86e36d.description: This operation fetches the details of a single user within a specified issue tracking collection. Use this endpoint when you need to access information about a particular user rather than a list of users. It reads user data associated with the given collection and user identifiers. Ensure the collection and user IDs are valid to avoid errors.

3d82fcf6f4c0dec8.summary: Update an existing SMS or MMS message's details
3d82fcf6f4c0dec8.description: Modify the properties of a specific SMS or MMS message, such as its sender, recipient, content, type, status, and scheduling information. Use this operation when you need to correct or change message attributes after creation, unlike sending a new message or retrieving message details. This action updates stored message data and may affect message delivery if status or scheduling fields are changed. Ensure that the message ID exists and that updates comply with allowed status transitions and field constraints.

0a28e5519d44b9fd.summary: Retrieve a specific ticket from a collection
0a28e5519d44b9fd.description: This operation fetches the details of a single ticket within a specified collection. Use this endpoint when you need to view or process information about one particular ticket rather than multiple tickets. It reads the ticket data without modifying it and requires valid collection and ticket identifiers. Ensure the ticket exists within the given collection to avoid errors.

9375b1bfb2592dd1.summary: Delete a specific ticket from an issue tracking collection
9375b1bfb2592dd1.description: This operation removes a ticket identified by its ID from a specified issue tracking collection. Use this endpoint when you need to permanently delete a ticket rather than updating or closing it. Deleting a ticket will remove all associated data and cannot be undone. Ensure the ticket exists in the collection before attempting deletion to avoid errors.

dcb342052f4bd79c.summary: Retrieve a list of all available APIs
dcb342052f4bd79c.description: This operation fetches a complete list of all APIs accessible through the connector. Use this to discover which APIs can be integrated or managed within the system. It reads the current API registry without modifying any data. There are no special constraints beyond standard access permissions.

0e7ce36aa6e5795f.summary: Delete a specific comment from a ticket in an issue tracking collection
0e7ce36aa6e5795f.description: This operation removes a single comment identified by its ID from a specified ticket within an issue tracking collection. Use this endpoint when you need to permanently delete a comment rather than editing or hiding it. Deleting a comment is irreversible and will remove it from all views and records associated with the ticket. Ensure the comment ID and ticket belong to the correct collection to avoid unintended data removal.

8f0dc78149e320a1.summary: Add a comment to a specific ticket in an issue tracking collection
8f0dc78149e320a1.description: This operation creates a new comment on a specified ticket within an issue tracking collection. Use this endpoint when you want to append additional information or discussion to an existing ticket. It writes the comment data, including content and metadata, to the ticket's comment thread. Ensure the ticket and collection IDs are valid, as the comment is linked directly to these entities.

6b1779b1e0728ecd.summary: Retrieve custom and default connection settings for a specific resource
6b1779b1e0728ecd.description: This operation fetches the custom configuration settings along with their default values required for a connection to a specified resource. Use this endpoint when you need to understand or modify the connection parameters for a particular resource within a service. It reads the current settings without making changes, ensuring you have the necessary details to manage connections effectively. The response includes both user-defined and default settings, helping to distinguish between customized and standard configurations.

dc8189d77cc6350a.summary: Retrieve a specific time off request for an employee
dc8189d77cc6350a.description: Fetch detailed information about a single time off request associated with a particular employee. Use this operation when you need to view or verify the details of an individual time off request rather than a list of requests. It reads the stored data for the specified request and employee, ensuring accurate retrieval of the request's status and details. Ensure the employee and request identifiers are valid to avoid errors.

10b92e19b58afd8b.summary: Retrieve details of a specific connector by its ID
10b92e19b58afd8b.description: This operation fetches detailed information about a single connector identified by its ID. Use this endpoint when you need to view or verify the configuration and status of a particular connector. It reads connector data without modifying any resources. Ensure the connector ID exists to avoid errors.

84551ebdc53eb09c.summary: Retrieve all tags for a specific issue tracking collection
84551ebdc53eb09c.description: Fetches the complete list of tags associated with a given issue tracking collection. Use this operation when you need to view or manage tags within a particular collection, as opposed to tags across multiple collections. This operation reads tag data without modifying it. Ensure the collection identifier is valid to receive the corresponding tags.

f62f79e017879883.summary: Proxy a downstream GET request with injected credentials for secure access
f62f79e017879883.description: This operation forwards a GET request to a downstream service while injecting credentials stored in Vault to enhance security. Use this when you need an additional security layer and logging without relying on Unify for normalization. It reads credentials from Vault and includes method and URL details in the headers. The proxy forwards all data transparently to the downstream service, ensuring secure and logged access without modifying the request payload.

10528a37931d50ef.summary: Retrieve all application consumers with their request counts
10528a37931d50ef.description: Fetches a list of all application consumers registered in the vault, including an aggregated count of requests each has made. Use this endpoint when you need a comprehensive overview of consumer activity and usage statistics. It reads existing consumer data and their request metrics without modifying any records. This operation provides a consolidated view but does not support filtering or pagination.

e604a9d69b48d182.summary: Create a temporary Hosted Vault session for managing integrations and settings
e604a9d69b48d182.description: This operation initiates a Hosted Vault session by generating a short-lived token and a URL for access. Use this endpoint when you need to grant a consumer temporary access to manage their integrations and settings through the Vault interface. The session token expires after one hour, so the consumer must use the provided URL promptly. This endpoint writes session data and returns a redirect URL but does not persist long-term credentials.

79973525e1e24bda.summary: Retrieve a specific API resource by its ID
79973525e1e24bda.description: This operation fetches the details of a single API resource identified by its resource ID within a given API. Use this endpoint when you need to view or inspect the configuration or metadata of a particular resource rather than listing all resources. It reads the resource data without modifying it and requires valid identifiers to locate the resource. If the resource does not exist, the operation will return an error indicating it was not found.

a094daf4da29e33b.summary: Delete a resource by proxying a downstream DELETE request with injected credentials
a094daf4da29e33b.description: This operation proxies a DELETE request to a downstream service while injecting credentials stored in Vault to ensure secure access. Use this when you want to add an extra security layer and logging without relying on Unify for request normalization. It forwards all data to the downstream URL and includes the method and verb in the headers. This method does not return a response body and requires proper credential setup in Vault to function correctly.

53b29a8b928c7eac.summary: Retrieve all tickets for a specific collection
53b29a8b928c7eac.description: Fetches a list of all tickets associated with the specified collection. Use this operation when you need to view or manage all tickets within a particular collection. It reads ticket data linked to the collection but does not modify any information. Ensure the collection ID is valid to receive the corresponding tickets.

f1dd975cf2aa0624.summary: Retrieve a specific resource from a connector
f1dd975cf2aa0624.description: This operation fetches the details of a single resource identified by its ID within a specified connector. Use this when you need to access information about one particular resource rather than a list of resources. It reads data associated with the resource but does not modify any connector or resource state. Ensure the connector and resource IDs are valid to avoid errors.

d8de048ff9ad421b.summary: Proxy a PATCH request to a downstream service with injected credentials
d8de048ff9ad421b.description: This operation forwards a PATCH request to a downstream service while injecting necessary credentials stored in Vault. Use this when you want to add an extra security layer and logging without relying on Unify for request normalization. It proxies all data to the downstream URL and includes the method and verb in the headers. Note that the request data is passed through as-is, and Vault handles credential injection transparently.

9013ed5b890201c4.summary: Import an authorized connection into the vault
9013ed5b890201c4.description: This operation imports an authorized connection for a specified service into the vault. Use this when you need to add existing credentials and settings to manage connections centrally. It writes the provided credentials, settings, and metadata into the vault, enabling secure storage and future access. Ensure the connection is authorized before importing to avoid errors.

3acd3e177ac0bbfb.summary: Update a webhook subscription and validate its delivery URL if changed
3acd3e177ac0bbfb.description: This operation updates an existing webhook subscription, allowing changes to its delivery URL, description, status, and subscribed events. Use this endpoint when you need to modify webhook settings, especially to update the delivery URL, which triggers a synchronous validation via an HTTP POST with an HMAC signature. If the delivery URL validation fails due to network issues, timeouts, or non-2xx responses, the webhook is updated but automatically disabled with a specific disabled reason. Always verify the webhook's status and disabled reason after updating to confirm it remains active.

04162f3fbbff9e6a.summary: Proxy a downstream POST request with injected credentials for secure forwarding
04162f3fbbff9e6a.description: This operation forwards a POST request to a downstream service while injecting credentials stored in Vault to enhance security. Use this when you need to add an extra security layer and logging without relying on Unify for data normalization. It forwards all data to the downstream URL and includes the original method and verb in the headers. The operation does not modify the request body beyond credential injection and requires that the downstream service accepts the proxied request format.

033271766e4dead2.summary: Delete a consumer and all associated connections
033271766e4dead2.description: This operation removes a specified consumer along with all their connections and credentials. Use this when you need to completely revoke a consumer's access and remove their related data from the system. Deleting a consumer is irreversible and will eliminate all credentials tied to that consumer. Ensure that this action is appropriate as it affects all linked connections and access rights.

7b58b9f1e6978fc6.summary: Retrieve detailed information about a specific consumer
7b58b9f1e6978fc6.description: This operation fetches detailed information about a consumer, including aggregated counts related to the connections they have authorized. Use this endpoint when you need to view a consumer's profile and their associated connection statistics. It reads data about the consumer and their authorized connections but does not modify any data. Ensure the consumer ID provided is valid to retrieve accurate information.

fe4744d631048821.summary: Retrieve all users associated with a specific issue tracking collection
fe4744d631048821.description: This operation fetches the complete list of users linked to a given issue tracking collection. Use this endpoint when you need to view or manage users within a particular collection, rather than users across all collections. It reads user data related to the specified collection and does not modify any information. Ensure the collection identifier is valid to receive accurate user listings.

330ce8e49b3af19c.summary: Forward OPTIONS requests to a downstream service with injected credentials
330ce8e49b3af19c.description: This operation forwards an OPTIONS HTTP request to a downstream service while injecting necessary credentials stored in Vault. Use this when you need to add a security layer and logging without relying on Unify for request normalization. It proxies all data to the downstream URL and includes the method and verb in the headers. This ensures secure and traceable communication with the downstream service during OPTIONS requests.

5d6d1eb065bb2a04.summary: Update an existing connection's details and configuration
5d6d1eb065bb2a04.description: This operation modifies the properties of an existing connection, such as its state, authentication type, status, and other metadata. Use this endpoint when you need to change connection settings without creating a new connection. It updates stored connection data and may affect how the connection interacts with integrated services. Ensure the connection identifiers are correct, as this operation targets a specific connection by unified API and service ID.

c4da779dc70f8748.summary: Retrieve a specific connection by unified API and service ID
c4da779dc70f8748.description: This operation fetches the details of a single connection identified by the unified API and service ID. Use this endpoint when you need to access information about a particular connection rather than a list of connections. It reads the stored connection data without modifying it. Ensure the provided identifiers are valid to successfully retrieve the connection.

60972f0284d59e84.summary: Retrieve all custom mappings for a specified unified API and service
60972f0284d59e84.description: This operation fetches a list of all custom mappings associated with a given unified API and service identifier. Use this endpoint when you need to view or manage the custom mappings configured for a particular service within the vault. It reads the stored mapping data without modifying it. Ensure the unified API and service ID are valid to receive the corresponding mappings.

3b1896563abac300.summary: Retrieve request counts for a consumer within a datetime range
3b1896563abac300.description: This operation fetches the number of requests made by a specific consumer over a specified datetime range. Use this endpoint when you need aggregated request statistics for monitoring or analysis purposes. It reads historical request data associated with the consumer but does not modify any stored information. Ensure the datetime range is valid to receive accurate counts.

98cb60fd2b8a6f8e.summary: Update the consent state of a vault connection
98cb60fd2b8a6f8e.description: This operation updates the consent status for a specified vault connection, indicating whether access is granted or revoked. Use this endpoint when you need to change the user's permission state for a connection managed within the vault. It modifies the consent data associated with the connection, affecting access to linked resources. Ensure the connection identifiers are correct, as the update applies only to the specified service and unified API combination.

bd8cd2d23f4d2ab0.summary: Delete a connection from the vault
bd8cd2d23f4d2ab0.description: This operation removes a specified connection from the vault. Use this when you need to permanently delete a connection and no longer require access to its stored credentials or configuration. Deleting a connection is irreversible and will remove all associated data for that connection. Ensure the connection is not in use before deletion to avoid disrupting dependent services.

ae1bcca1772377d8.summary: Retrieve custom fields for a connection resource
ae1bcca1772377d8.description: This operation fetches all custom fields associated with a specified connection resource. Use this endpoint when you need to view or manage metadata fields that are uniquely defined for a resource within a vault connection. It reads the current custom field configurations without modifying any data. Ensure the resource and connection identifiers are valid to obtain accurate custom field information.

a189e452ad734d70.summary: Validate the current state of a connection and refresh tokens if needed
a189e452ad734d70.description: This operation checks the validity of a specified connection by performing different validations depending on its authentication type. Use this to verify that required fields exist for basic and API key authentications, or to trigger an OAuth2 access token refresh regardless of expiry. It does not accept credentials in the request body and does not persist any changes, only validating the connection state. If a token refresh occurs successfully, the updated access token will be used for future API calls.

5c97d175793fbf81.summary: Retrieve coverage details for a specific API resource
5c97d175793fbf81.description: This operation fetches the coverage information of a particular resource within an API. Use this endpoint when you need to assess how well a resource is covered, such as for testing or monitoring purposes. It reads coverage data associated with the specified resource but does not modify any data. Ensure the resource and API identifiers are valid to receive accurate coverage details.

10eb7b75c3a4c862.summary: Update default settings for a connection's resource
10eb7b75c3a4c862.description: This operation modifies the default configuration values for a specific resource within a connection. Use it when you need to change connection parameters such as authentication type, state, or status without creating a new connection. It updates stored settings that affect how the connection interacts with the resource, ensuring future requests use the new configuration. Note that this operation only changes default values and does not affect existing active sessions or data.

8629154915d1cbe5.summary: Create a callback state for issuing requests to a callback endpoint
8629154915d1cbe5.description: This operation creates a callback state that enables secure interactions with a callback endpoint. Use this endpoint when you need to establish a state token to manage callback requests within the vault connections. It writes a new callback state associated with the specified service and unified API, facilitating controlled callback communications. Ensure the redirect URI is valid as it is part of the callback state creation process.

22e7b31fe02668b9.summary: Create a webhook subscription to receive specified events
22e7b31fe02668b9.description: This operation creates a webhook subscription that delivers specified events to a given URL. Use this to set up event notifications via HTTP POST requests to your endpoint. The delivery URL is validated synchronously by sending a signed POST request; if validation fails, the webhook is created but disabled with a reason indicating validation failure. Always verify the webhook's status and disabled reason in the response to confirm it is active and receiving events.

5852a140b8719501.summary: Retrieve a list of all webhook event logs
5852a140b8719501.description: This operation fetches all recorded webhook event logs. Use it to review the history of webhook events for monitoring or debugging purposes. It reads stored event data without modifying any records. There are no special constraints beyond standard access permissions.

6bb36f164425f4fc.summary: Retrieve all custom mappings for a specific connection
6bb36f164425f4fc.description: This operation fetches the complete list of custom mappings associated with a given connection. Use this endpoint when you need to view or audit the custom mappings configured for a particular unified API, service, and resource combination. It reads the stored mapping configurations without modifying any data. Ensure the specified connection identifiers are valid to receive the correct mappings.

f0b8f545bd1670b7.summary: Retrieve all consumer request logs from the vault
f0b8f545bd1670b7.description: This operation fetches all consumer request logs stored in the vault. Use this endpoint when you need a comprehensive view of all logged consumer requests, unlike more specific log retrieval endpoints that filter by criteria. It reads all available log entries without modifying any data. There are no special constraints beyond standard access permissions.

030ec17a394b6c95.summary: Delete a webhook subscription by its ID
030ec17a394b6c95.description: This operation removes an existing webhook subscription identified by its ID. Use this to stop receiving events or notifications from the webhook source. Deleting a webhook subscription permanently disables its event delivery and cannot be undone. Ensure the ID corresponds to an active subscription to avoid errors.

6046a4df94c461c7.summary: Update a consumer's metadata including name and email
6046a4df94c461c7.description: This operation updates the metadata of an existing consumer, such as their name and email address. Use this endpoint when you need to modify consumer details without affecting other consumer attributes. It writes changes directly to the consumer's stored metadata. Ensure the consumer exists before attempting to update, as this operation does not create new consumers.

f589ea691bd1fec5.summary: Retrieve all consent records for a specific connection
f589ea691bd1fec5.description: This operation fetches all consent records associated with a given connection identified by unified API and service ID. Use this endpoint when you need to review or audit all consents granted for a particular connection. It reads consent data without modifying any records. Ensure the connection identifiers are valid to retrieve the correct consent information.

efb615e90e3fe9a8.summary: Retrieve details of a specific webhook subscription
efb615e90e3fe9a8.description: This operation fetches the details of a webhook subscription identified by its ID. Use this endpoint when you need to view the configuration or status of an existing webhook subscription. It reads the stored subscription data without modifying it. Ensure the specified ID corresponds to an existing webhook subscription to avoid errors.

bef58d0d99bdcbe9.summary: Retrieve a list of all webhook subscriptions
bef58d0d99bdcbe9.description: This operation fetches all existing webhook subscriptions configured in the system. Use it when you need to view or manage the current webhooks rather than creating or deleting them. It reads the stored webhook subscription data without modifying it. There are no special constraints beyond standard access permissions.
